The Airline That Introduced Lie-Flat Business Class Seats To The World

Find out everything you need to know about the airlines that introduced lie-flat business class seats to the world.

Lie-flat business class became a premium differentiator that fundamentally reshaped long-haul competition and revenue models across the industry.

Military Mar 26, 2026

Golden Dome and the velocity race: Why ground-based optics are the key to mission persistence

While often described as a moonshot for missile defense, the Golden Dome for America mission is in reality something even more challenging: an exercise in disciplined systems engineering under threat. Hypersonic glide vehicles, maneuvering ballistic missiles and complex decoys are compressing...

Ground-based optical tracking systems are becoming critical infrastructure for detecting and tracking hypersonic threats that exceed traditional radar capabilities.

Business Aviation Mar 26, 2026

Bombardier delivers first Global 8000 to NetJets

Bombardier has handed over the first Global 8000 business jet to NetJets, marking the fleet launch customer’s entry…...

NetJets' adoption of the Global 8000 signals strong fractional ownership demand for ultra-long-range jets despite economic uncertainty in the business aviation market.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Up To 17-Hour Nonstop: Emirates' 10 Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Emirates is the world's second-largest long-haul operator, after United Airlines. In 2026, the carrier has an average of 134 daily long-haul passenger departures from its Dubai hub. Over half of the carrier's total flights fall into this category.

Emirates' ultra-long routes requiring 17-hour flights demonstrate how hub consolidation in Dubai enables profitability on point-to-point services that smaller carriers cannot sustainably operate.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

United Airlines transforms devil’s chariot into premium CRJ-450

LOS ANGELES — Before the COVID-19 pandemic upended the airline industry, one-third of all United Airlines’ domestic US departures were on 50-seat regional jets. While that number has fallen to just 10% today, United isn’t resting on its laurels as demand for premium travel products remains high....

United's redeployment of surplus regional jets into premium-configured aircraft captures higher-yield passengers while managing post-pandemic capacity shifts.

See Runway Girl Network →

South Korea's transition from prototype to mass production of the KF-21 signals regional air superiority capabilities and reduces dependence on foreign fighter imports.

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI Avata 360 global launch begins, US delayed again

DJI has kicked off the global rollout of the Avata 360, positioning it as its most immersive consumer drone yet. In many parts of the world, that means orders are either already live or pre-sales have begun. In the US, though, things are playing out a little differently, again. more…...

DJI's continued US market exclusion from its latest consumer drone releases reflects ongoing regulatory barriers that are fragmenting global product launch timelines and creating competitive advantages for alternative manufacturers.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Up To 16-Hour Nonstop Flights: Etihad Airways' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Two of the routes are brand-new this year, while another started in 2025. Learn more here!

Etihad's expansion of ultra-long routes indicates growing market demand for direct connectivity between Middle Eastern hubs and distant markets, potentially reshaping competitive dynamics in long-haul aviation.

Military Mar 26, 2026

First Production KF-21 Boramae Rolled Out of Factory in South Korea

The first serial production KF-21 Boramae fighter jet has rolled out of the Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) factory in a major milestone for the program. The first of 40 serial production KF-21 Boramae fighter jets has rolled out of the Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) factory, marking a major...

South Korea's indigenous fighter program moves toward operational deployment, reducing regional dependence on foreign fighter acquisitions and establishing domestic aerospace manufacturing capability.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to test nuclear electric propulsion with 2028 mission to Mars

NASA’s new exploration strategy includes the rapid development of a nuclear-powered mission to Mars in 2028, leveraging hardware originally built for the lunar Gateway.

Nuclear electric propulsion development accelerates Mars timelines by repurposing existing lunar hardware, reducing development costs and risk.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

A350-900 vs 777-300ER: Who Wins on Range?

For more than two decades, the Boeing 777-300ER has been one of the most recognizable long-haul aircraft in the world. It has carried millions of passengers across continents, linking global hubs like Dubai, New York, Hong Kong, and Los Angeles with remarkable reliability. Airlines have long...

The A350-900's superior fuel efficiency enables equivalent range to the 777-300ER with 80+ fewer seats, fundamentally shifting airline economics for long-haul operations.

Frederick Municipal is a small general and corporate aviation airport located just east of the city of Frederick, Maryland. The traffic mix runs the spectrum from single engine training aircraft up...

Regional airports like Frederick serve as critical training hubs and economic anchors for small communities while supporting diverse aircraft operations from trainers to corporate jets.

Russia denies sending modified Shahed drones to Iran

Russia is reportedly close to completing shipments of modified Shahed-type drones to Iran, according to Western intelligence cited…...

Russia's drone technology transfer to Iran creates a competitive threat loop where adversaries gain access to modified platforms that may be reverse-engineered into regional air defense challenges.

FlyFocus unveils KURIER unmanned helicopter

FlyFocus, a Warsaw-based defence technology company specialising in unmanned aerial systems (UAS) and avionics, has...

FlyFocus's KURIER helicopter represents Poland's growing capability in autonomous defense platforms, signaling increased European competition in the unmanned rotorcraft market.

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation segments offers airlines and airports a faster, lower-cost path to emissions reduction than waiting for next-generation aircraft to deploy fleet-wide.

See MDPI →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Video Shows Super Hornet’s Near Miss After Apparent Iranian MANPADS Launch

A U.S. F/A-18 Super Hornet was involved in a near miss after being targeted by an apparent Iranian MANPADS system during a low altitude strafing run with the 20 mm M61 cannon. Videos emerged online on Mar. 26, 2026, show a U.S. Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet fighter jet targeted by an apparent Iranian...

Low-altitude cannon runs remain tactically vulnerable to portable air-defense systems, highlighting persistent risks in contested airspace.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 26, 2026

The Top 5 Highest Paid US Air Force Pilot Ranks & Their Average 2026 Salaries

Air Force pilots are paid based on their rank and years of service, although even the highest ranks typically earn less than commercial pilots.

Military pilots face significant compensation disadvantage versus commercial counterparts, potentially affecting recruitment and retention of experienced talent.

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI Avata 360 drone launch delayed in US

DJI’s 360° drone launch may already be underway globally, but US buyers could be stuck waiting. A newly updated banner on DJI’s official Amazon US storefront now points to March 30 at 8 a.m. EST as the release timing for its upcoming DJI Avata 360 drone, subtly overriding the March 26 worldwide...

DJI's US market launch delay for the Avata 360 suggests ongoing regulatory or supply chain friction specific to American operations despite global availability.

DJI's aggressive pricing strategy signals intensifying competition in the consumer drone market, potentially reshaping consumer expectations and competitor margins across the industry.

Why The World’s Most-Produced Fighter Jet May Finally Disappear In 2026

Why the last combat-worthy MiG-21s likely disappeared in 2025, with only on-paper rusting hulks in service in 2026.

The MiG-21's retirement marks a critical capability gap for smaller air forces that lack advanced fighter alternatives or modernization funding.

Nota del editor: La NASA actualizará continuamente esta página de sesiones informativas y eventos de la misión Artemis II a lo largo de las actividades previas al lanzamiento, el lanzamiento y las operaciones de la misión. La NASA ofrecerá cobertura en vivo de los eventos previos al lanzamiento, el...

NASA's comprehensive media coverage strategy for Artemis II aims to maintain public engagement and transparency throughout the lunar mission's launch and operational phases.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Ames Experts Available for Artemis II Flight Test Interviews

NASA’s Ames Research Center in Silicon Valley invites media to interview local subject matter experts on Friday, March 27 from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. ahead of the agency sending astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years with the Artemis II flight test. NASA teams are gearing up...

NASA Ames is positioning regional expertise as a public engagement strategy to build awareness of critical lunar mission capabilities before Artemis II's historic crewed flight.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Sets Coverage for First Artemis Crewed Mission Around Moon

A variety of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for NASA’s Artemis II mission around the Moon will stream online. The agency is targeting no earlier than Wednesday, April 1, for the test flight during a two-hour window that opens at 6:24 p.m. EDT, with additional launch opportunities through...

NASA's April 2025 Artemis II launch window provides aerospace manufacturers and supply chain partners a critical deadline for final component validation and integration testing.
